announcement_body.headline: 7.41d Gameplay Patch
announcement_body.body:
[p]Patch 7.41d is out now and you can check out the patch notes [url="https://www.dota2.com/patches/7.41d"]here[/url].[/p]

In addition, we've recently addressed the following issues:
[list]
[*] Fixed Greevils not spawning their equipped effects
[*] Fixed Earthshaker's Planetfall Arcana not tracking combos and combo damage properly
[*] Fixed right click on Dota Labs Overlay Map not working when "Auto-Repeat Right Mouse" is enabled
[*] Fixed Bottle with a stored Rune being able to be moved in and out of the backpack
[*] Fixed Harmonizer not considering abilities with charges on cooldown
[*] Fixed Elder Titan's Astral Spirit's movement speed not taking Wind Lace into account
[*] Fixed Elder Titan's Echo Stomp shard not swapping places with Astral Spirit if he is being moved by another ability when the stomp goes off
[*] Fixed Invoker not receiving an Aghanim's Blessing from Alchemist if Alchemist casts Aghanim's Scepter on Invoker when his main inventory is full but the backpack had space
[*] Fixed Alchemist being able to repeatedly give Invoker Aghanim's Blessings if Invoker hasn't chosen his Aghanim's Scepter upgrade
[*] Fixed Invoker not correctly being refunded for his Aghanim's Blessing if Invoker has not selected an Aghanim's Scepter upgrade and Alchemist casts Aghanim's Scepter on him
[*] Fixed Spirit Bear's permanently consumed items not counting towards Lone Druid's net worth
[*] Fixed Spirit Breaker permanently changing teams when he reflects Venomancer's Noxious Plague with Lotus Orb and then casts Planar Pocket on Venomancer before Noxious Plague spreads, then after it spreads to a neutral creep Venomancer uses Helm of the Dominator on that creep
[*] Fixed Venomancer being able to Transmute Spirit Breaker with Hand of Midas through the same process
[*] Fixed Wraith King's Vampiric Spirit not turning Wraith King into a wraith if he levels up at the same time as dying
[*] Ability Draft: Fixed Forge Spirit starting at too high a level
[*] Ability Draft: Fixed Brewmaster's old Aghanim's Scepter being available
[/list]

announcement_body.headline: The International 2026 Ticket Sales
announcement_body.body:
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/3703047/882f6398bbe0c283cb33d57a3c18160c5d5a9f00.png[/img]

In just a few short months, the finest Dota teams in the world will gather in Shanghai to battle for the Aegis of Champions in front of thousands of cheering fans. We've announced [url=https://www.dota2.com/newsentry/655981180194128453]the official team invitations[/url], and now we'd like to extend one to you. If you want to be one of those fans cheering live at the arena, it's time to get ready: ticket sales for The International 2026 kick off on June 10th at 2pm China Standard Time.

The in-arena action runs from August 20 - 23 at the SPD Bank Oriental Sports Center, with two ticket options available: single-day tickets for any day, or two-day bundles covering Thursday/Friday and Saturday/Sunday. All seating this year will be assigned, with no General Admission options available.

Ticket prices vary based on day and section, so check out the full chart of options below:

[img]{STEAM_CLAN_LOC_IMAGE}/3703047/eeda1e52cc7d32f4c0db01e2ed4f7ff409a95c4f.png[/img]

There are two purchase paths this year: one for fans purchasing domestically within China, and another for purchasing internationally.

[h2]China Domestic Sales[/h2]
Chinese domestic ticket sales will be available through [url=https://m.damai.cn/shows/item.html?itemId=1052581844801]Damai[/url] or the Perfect World app, and will require a Chinese ID to complete any sale.

Once ticket sales begin on June 10th at 2pm (China Standard Time), the first twenty minutes will serve as a priority purchase period for domestic tickets. During this period, purchases are restricted to active Dota players in China who have played at least ten games in within the last six months. (To be eligible for priority purchase, please [url=https://www.dota2.com.cn/international/2026/ticketspriority]verify your account status[/url] in advance by the end of Wednesday, June 3, 2026.)

From 2:30pm (CST) onward, domestic tickets will be available for purchase to all Chinese customers.

[h2]International Sales[/h2]
For fans purchasing outside of China, ticket sales will commence on June 10th at 2:30pm (CST) via [url=https://www.trip.com/t/TI2026]Trip.com[/url].

[h1]FAQ[/h1]

[b]How does seating work?[/b]
Tickets are purchased for a specific seating region (see chart above). After your purchase, you will be assigned a specific seat in the region you selected. All seats are assigned and reserved. [i]There are no general admission tickets or seats.[/i]

[b]What is the ticket transfer policy?[/b]
Tickets cannot be transferred, gifted, or resold. Tickets can only be used by the names for which they were purchased.

[b]What do I need to purchase tickets internationally?[/b]
Trip.com requires an account and a valid passport for international ticket purchases. You can create your account and submit attendee documentation before tickets go on sale.

[b]Can I purchase tickets for my group?[/b]
Yes, but note that the ticketing platforms limit each individual account to a maximum of two tickets per day. Multiple transactions may be required depending on the size of your group and how many days you wish to attend. As noted above, each attendee/ticket holder must have proper identification in order to complete the checkout process.

[b]Do I need to bring anything else besides my tickets to enter the event?[/b]
Event entrance requires a valid photo ID matching the name on the ticket.

[b]What is the ticket refund policy?[/b]
For China domestic ticket sales, please see the [url=https://m.damai.cn/shows/item.html?itemId=1052581844801]Damai refund policy[/url]. For international ticket sales, please see the [url=https://www.trip.com/t/TI2026]Trip.com refund policy[/url].

announcement_body.headline: The International 2026 Invitations and Qualifiers
announcement_body.body:
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/3703047/1477d1a3748ef268c4b431dd4af64cf4ad8ffdd7.png[/img]

[p]Throughout the year, Dota teams around the world battle to prove themselves worthy of direct invitation to The International — and a chance to compete for the Aegis of Champions on the world's biggest stage. Teams that secure invites can begin to focus on their final preparations, while the remaining contenders face one last chance to fight their way in.[/p]

[h3]Direct Invites[/h3]
[p]With less than three months until we raise the curtains on the big show, we’re excited to extend these seven teams invitations to Shanghai, China to compete in The International 2026:[/p]

[list]
[*]Aurora Gaming
[expand type=details]
Egor "Nightfall" Grigorenko
Rafli "Mikoto" Fathur Rahman
Wei Shen "Ws`" Chung
Myroslav "Mira" Kolpakov
Oleh "kaori" Medvedok
[/expand]

[*]BoomBoys
[expand type=details]
Ilia "Kiritych~" Ulianov
Danil "gpk~" Skutin
Matvei "MieRo`" Vasiunin
Vitalie "Save-" Melnic
Vladislav "Kataomi`" Semenov
[/expand]

[*]Team Falcons
[expand type=details]
Oliver "Skiter" Lepko
Stanislav "Malr1ne" Potorak
Ammar "ATF" Alassaf
Andreas "Cr1t-" Nielsen
Jingjun "Sneyking" Wu
[/expand]

[*]Team Liquid
[expand type=details]
Michael "m1CKe" Vu
Michał "Nisha" Jankowski
Marcus "Ace" Christensen
Samuel "Boxi" Svahn
Erik "tOfu" Engel
[/expand]

[*]Tundra Esports
[expand type=details]
Ivan "Pure" Moskalenko
Bozhidar "bzm" Bogdanov
Neta "33" Shapira
Matthew "Ari" Walker
Matthew "Whitemon" Filemon
[/expand]

[*]Xtreme Gaming
[expand type=details]
Chunyu "Ame" Wang
Jin Xiang "NothingToSay" Cheng
Jing "Xxs" Lin
Linsen "fy" Xu
Jian Wei "xNova" Yap
[/expand]

[*]Team Yandex
[expand type=details]
Alimzhan "医者watson" Islambekov
Ilya "CHIRA_JUNIOR" Chirtsov
Dmitrii "DM" Dorokhin
Martin "Saksa" Sazdov
Arman "Malady" Orazbayev
[/expand]
[/list]

[h3]Regional Qualifiers[/h3][p]
The seven teams above have already earned invitations, which means there are still nine slots available at TI, just waiting for the squads that can survive the crucible of Regional Qualifiers. This year, we've merged the Eastern and Western Europe qualifiers into one single European qualifier with four advancement slots. Check out the full schedule below.[/p]

[list][*][b]China[/b], June 15-18 (2 slots)
[expand type=details]
Vici Gaming
Roar Gaming
Yakutou Brothers
Team Resilience
[i](+4 teams from Open Qualifiers)[/i]
[/expand]

[*][b]South America[/b], June 15-19 (1 slot)
[expand type=details]
LGD Gaming
PlayTime
Estar Backs
Red Hot Chili Pibble
Amaru Gaming
Hokori
[i](+4 teams from Open Qualifiers)[/i]
[/expand]

[*][b]Southeast Asia[/b], June 19-23 (1 slot)
[expand type=details]
REKONIX
OG
Execration
Team Nemesis
GLYPH
[i](+5 teams from Open Qualifiers)[/i]
[/expand]

[*][b]Europe[/b], June 21-28 (4 slots)
[expand type=details]
TEAM VISION
Team Spirit
MOUZ
Natus Vincere
Nigma Galaxy
Virtus.pro
enjoy
Power Rangers
[i](+8 teams from Open Qualifiers)[/i]
[/expand]

[*][b]North America[/b], June 24-26 (1 slot)
[expand type=details]
GamerLegion
[i](+3 teams from Open Qualifiers)[/i]
[/expand]
[/list]

[p]Once qualifiers wrap up on June 28, we'll have the full tournament field decided, and the next stop is Shanghai. Group Stage kicks off August 13 - 16 with a three-day, five-round Swiss bracket, followed by five elimination matches played on Sunday. The eight remaining teams bring the competition into the arena August 20 - 23, where the Aegis of Champions awaits.[/p]
